indication: Metastatic or locally advance neuroendocrine tumours irrespective of primary site, predominantly pancreas but also bowel and lung. Palliative intent. WHO performance status 0, 1, 2

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Start on the evening of day 1 of the cycle. Take with or after food. Capecitabine will be dose banded in accordance with national dose bands.
Vesicant. Streptozocin will be dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (100mg/ml).

pre_medications.special_instructions: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy; oral or intravenous bolus
15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy; oral or intravenous bolus

take_home_medicines.special_instructions: Start on day 2 of the cycle
Take in the morning with or after food. Start on day 2 of the cycle. For 3 days.
Please supply an original pack as appropriate
Starting on the evening of day 1 of treatment

dose_modifications.notes: 100% dose
First occurrence: delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover continue with 100% capecitabine dose and 80% streptozocin dose.
First occurrence: delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover continue with 100% capecitabine dose and 80% streptozocin dose.
Second occurrence: delay for 7 days. If counts recover continue with 80% of the original dose for both agents.
Third occurrence: delay for 7 days. If counts recover continue with capecitabine at 80% and streptozocin at 60% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic toxicity grade 2 or less (ALT/AST 3.0-5.0xULN, bilirubin 1.5-3xULN) - first appearance
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until grade 1 or below (ALT/AST 3xULN or less, bilirubin 1.5xULN or less) then restart both drugs at 100% doses.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic toxicity grade 3 (ALT/AST 5-20xULN, bilirubin 3.0-10xULN) - first appearance
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until grade 1 or less, then retreat at: 100% capecitabine dose and 80% streptozocin dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic toxicity grade 2 or below - second appearance
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until grade 1 or below then restart at 100% doses of both drugs.
Delay until grade 1 or below, then retreat at: 80% capecitabine dose and 80% streptozocin dose.
100% of original dose.
80% of original dose for both capecitabine and streptozocin.
60% of original dose for both capecitabine and streptozocin.
Discontinue both agents.
There is a lack of information available. In patients with mild to moderate hepatic dysfunction due to liver metastases, 100% of the dose is probably acceptable.
Consider dose modification.
Interrupt treatment until toxicity resolves to NCI-CTC grade 0-1 then continue at the same dose.
If toxicity recurs a second time interrupt treatment until resolves to NCI-CTC grade 0-1 then resume therapy at 75% of the original dose.
If same adverse effect develops on third occasion interrupt until resolves to NCI-CTC grade 0-1 then continue at 50% of original dose. Stop if toxicity re-appears fourth time.
Interrupt until resolves to NCI-CTC grade 0-1 then continue at 75% of original dose with prophylaxis if appropriate.
If toxicity recurs a second time interrupt until resolves to grade 0-1 and resume at 50% of original dose.
If same adverse effect develops on third occasion discontinue capecitabine.
Discontinue treatment unless consultant considers it in patient's best interest to continue at 50% of original dose once toxicity has resolved to grade 0-1.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance prior to each cycle. Consider blood transfusion if patient symptomatic of anaemia or has haemoglobin less than 8g/dL.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs (including albumin) and U&Es prior to each cycle
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance prior to each cycle
Regular monitoring of blood glucose levels
Urinalysis for signs of proteinuria and glycosuria prior to each cycle
All patients should be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation (cycle 1) to minimise the risk of severe and fatal toxicity with capecitabine
special_warnings: Patients with complete or partial dihydropyrimidine dehydrogenase (DPD) deficiency are at increased risk of severe and fatal toxicity during treatment with capecitabine. All patients should be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation.
Streptozocin is a vesicant.
In up to 20% of patients there may be a sudden release of insulin with hypoglycaemia occurring within 24 hours of treatment. BMs should be monitored during treatment.

Severe diarrhoea and/or severe mucositis early in the first treatment cycle can be the first presenting toxicity due to DPD enzyme deficiency, in which case potentially fatal neutropenia can quickly follow.

Ensure the total daily dose of capecitabine is divided into two doses given twelve hours apart (first dose administered in the evening of day one of the cycle). Serious toxicity has occurred where total daily dose has been given twice a day.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed when prescribing, dispensing or administering oral chemotherapy.
If chest pain occurs consider stopping capecitabine.
In general when a dose of capecitabine has been reduced it should not be increased at a later date. When capecitabine is stopped for toxicity the doses are omitted, not delayed.

required_patient_fields.creatinine_clearance: Required for renal dose modification - EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance prior to each cycle

drugs.special_instructions: Start on the evening of day 1 of the cycle. Take with or after food. Capecitabine will be dose banded in accordance with national dose bands.
drugs.notes: twice daily | Start on the evening of day 1 of the cycle. Take with or after food. Capecitabine will be dose banded in accordance with national dose bands.

drugs.special_instructions: Vesicant. Streptozocin will be dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (100mg/ml).
drugs.notes: once | Vesicant. Streptozocin will be dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (100mg/ml).

indication: The first or second line treatment of moderately differentiated PNET carcinomas. Performance status 0, 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Take at the same time of day every day with or without food, but not after a high fat meal. If a dose is missed do not take an additional dose but rather take the next scheduled dose. Available as 2.5mg, 5mg and 10mg tablets. Please supply an original pack per 28 day cycle.

dose_modifications.notes: Full dose.
1st Occurrence: Interrupt treatment until recovery to 1x10^9/L or greater then restart at the full dose.
2nd Occurrence: Interrupt treatment until recovery to 1x10^9/L or greater then restart at 5mg once a day.

dose_modifications.condition: < 0.5 x10^9/L or NCI-CTC grade 3 febrile neutropenia (first occurrence)
dose_modifications.notes: 1st Occurrence: Interrupt treatment until the fever has resolved and neutrophils are 1x10^9/L or greater then restart at 5mg once a day.

dose_modifications.condition: < 0.5 x10^9/L or NCI-CTC grade 3 febrile neutropenia (second occurrence)
dose_modifications.notes: 2nd Occurrence: Discontinue treatment permanently.
Discontinue treatment permanently.
Full dose.
1st Occurrence: Interrupt treatment until recovery to 75x10^9/L or greater then restart at the full dose.
2nd Occurrence: Interrupt treatment until recovery to 75x10^9/L or greater then restart at 5mg once a day.
1st Occurrence: Interrupt treatment until recovery to 75x10^9/L or greater then restart at 5mg once a day.
2nd Occurrence: Discontinue treatment permanently.
Discontinue treatment permanently.
10mg once a day (full dose).
5mg once a day.
Not recommended.
No dose modification required for renal impairment.
Continue everolimus without dose adjustments if patient can tolerate it. Alternatively interrupt until symptoms resolve to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below and then re-challenge at the same dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Mucositis NCI-CTC Grade 3 or recurrence of grade 2
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt treatment until symptoms have resolved to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below then restart everolimus at 5mg once a day.

dose_modifications.condition: Mucositis NCI-CTC Grade 4 or recurrence of grade 3
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue everolimus.
Continue everolimus without dose adjustments.
Interrupt therapy. Consider short term use of corticosteroids e.g. prednisolone 20mg once a day for 10-14 days. Restart everolimus at 5mg once a day when symptoms have resolved.
Interrupt therapy. Prescribe corticosteroids e.g. prednisolone 40mg as indicated. Restart everolimus at 5mg daily once symptoms have resolved or discontinue as appropriate.
Discontinue the everolimus. Treat appropriately.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: 28 day cycle continued as long as clinical benefit is observed or until unacceptable toxicity occurs.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, U&Es and LFTs every 4 weeks
Blood glucose levels at baseline and after 4 weeks of treatment, thereafter every 4-8 weeks
Triglycerides at baseline then every 8 weeks
special_warnings: Everolimus interacts with many other agents. Always check for drug interactions.
Everolimus should only be prescribed under the supervision of an oncologist.
Mouthcare for prophylaxis or treatment of mucositis - avoid mouthwashes containing alcohols.
National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy (NPSA/2008/RRR001) must be followed.
Consider blood transfusion or prescription of erythropoietin according to NICE TA 323 if patient is symptomatic of anaemia or has haemoglobin less than 8g/dL.
Gastric protection with a proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.

required_patient_fields.bilirubin: Required for hepatic dose modification (Child Pugh classification)

drugs.special_instructions: Take at the same time of day every day with or without food, but not after a high fat meal. If a dose is missed do not take an additional dose but rather take the next scheduled dose. Available as 2.5mg, 5mg and 10mg tablets. Please supply an original pack per 28 day cycle.
drugs.notes: once daily | Take at the same time of day every day with or without food, but not after a high fat meal. If a dose is missed do not take an additional dose but rather take the next scheduled dose. Available as 2.5mg, 5mg and 10mg tablets. Please supply an original pack per 28 day cycle.

indication: The treatment of differentiated thyroid cancer after radioactive iodine where all the following criteria are met: confirmed histological diagnosis of differentiated thyroid carcinoma (papillary or follicular or Hurthle cell type); metastatic disease or inoperable locally advanced disease; disease refractory to radioactive iodine; disease is progressive and either symptomatic or imminently likely to become symptomatic; patient is treatment naive to both lenvatinib and sorafenib (with specific exceptions). WHO Performance status 0, 1, 2. Palliative intent.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Take at the same time of day each day consistently with or without food. Capsules should be swallowed whole. If a dose is missed and cannot be taken within twelve hours then it should be missed and the next dose taken at the appropriate time. Available as 10mg and 4mg hard capsules. Please supply an original pack per 28 day cycle. There are several brands of lenvatinib - dispense the correct brand.

take_home_medicines.special_instructions: Oral chemotherapy. Please supply an original pack per 28 day cycle. Ensure product dispensed is appropriate for the indication.

dose_modifications.notes: 24mg once a day.
20mg once a day.
14mg once a day.
10mg once a day. If a third dose reduction is necessary treatment should be stopped.
No dose adjustment.
No dose adjustment.
14mg once a day. Further dose reductions may be required based on tolerability.
14mg once a day. Further dose reductions may be required based on tolerability.

dose_modifications.condition: Hypertension: systolic BP >= 140mmHg and up to 160mmHg or diastolic BP >= 90mmHg up to 100mmHg
dose_modifications.notes: Continue lenvatinib and initiate antihypertensive therapy, or if already receiving, increase dose or initiate additional antihypertensive therapy.

dose_modifications.condition: Hypertension: systolic BP >= 160mmHg or diastolic BP >= 100mmHg despite optimal antihypertensive therapy
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold lenvatinib until systolic BP <= 150mmHg and diastolic BP <= 95mmHg and patient on stable antihypertensive for at least 48 hours, then resume at reduced dose level.

dose_modifications.condition: Hypertension: life-threatening consequences (malignant hypertension, neurological deficit, or hypertensive crisis)
dose_modifications.notes: Urgent intervention indicated. Discontinue lenvatinib and institute appropriate medical management.
Interrupt treatment until proteinuria < 2g/24 hours, then dose reduce down a level and resume. Lenvatinib should be stopped if nephrotic syndrome occurs.
Lenvatinib should be stopped when QT interval is longer than 500ms. Treatment may be resumed at a reduced dose level when interval is 480ms or less.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: No formal haematological delay thresholds specified. Formal medical review at end of first 8 weeks of treatment. No treatment breaks of more than 6 weeks beyond expected cycle length allowed. Blood pressure should be well controlled prior to starting treatment.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, U&Es and LFTs every 28 days
Blood glucose levels at baseline and after 28 days of treatment, thereafter every 4-8 weeks
Triglycerides at baseline then every 8 weeks
Proteinuria less than 1g/24 hours, with proteinuria measured prior to each cycle
Thyroid function every 28 days
Blood pressure monitored at baseline, after one week of lenvatinib then every two weeks for first two months and then every 28 days thereafter
ECG as clinically indicated
special_warnings: Lenvatinib interacts with many other agents. Always check for drug interactions.
There are several brands of lenvatinib - please dispense the correct brand appropriate for the indication.
Lenvatinib impairs exogenous thyroid suppression - TSH levels should be monitored and thyroid hormone administration adjusted to reach appropriate TSH levels.

QT prolongation reported - may lead to severe ventricular arrhythmias including Torsades de pointes. Not recommended in patients with congenital long QT syndrome or risk factors for prolonged QT. Electrolyte abnormalities should be corrected prior to starting.
Renal impairment and renal failure reported - primary risk factor is dehydration/hypovolaemia due to gastrointestinal toxicity. Gastrointestinal toxicity should be actively managed.
Hepatic failure and acute hepatitis (less than 1%) have been reported. Liver function tests should be monitored before initiation, every 2 weeks for first 2 months, then monthly.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed. Lenvatinib should only be prescribed under supervision of an oncologist.
Routine anti-emetics are not required.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.

required_patient_fields.creatinine_clearance: Required for renal dose modification (dose reduction to 14mg if CrCl < 30ml/min)
required_patient_fields.bilirubin: Required for hepatic dose modification (Child Pugh classification)

drugs.special_instructions: Take at the same time of day each day consistently with or without food. Capsules should be swallowed whole. If a dose is missed and cannot be taken within twelve hours then it should be missed and the next dose taken at the appropriate time. Available as 10mg and 4mg hard capsules. Please supply an original pack per 28 day cycle. There are several brands of lenvatinib - dispense the correct brand.
drugs.notes: once daily | Take at the same time of day each day consistently with or without food. Capsules should be swallowed whole. If a dose is missed and cannot be taken within twelve hours then it should be missed and the next dose taken at the appropriate time. Available as 10mg and 4mg hard capsules. Please supply an original pack per 28 day cycle. There are several brands of lenvatinib - dispense the correct brand.

indication: Metastatic or locally advance neuroendocrine tumours

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Minimum 120 minutes. Maximum rate 1mg cisplatin/minute. Exfoliant. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (1mg/ml). Requires pre and post hydration.
Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Inflammantant. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (25mg/ml PM). Patients should be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation.
Intravenous infusion in 250ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes.
Vesicant. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (100mg/ml).

pre_medications.special_instructions: Given first on day 1, in 250ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes, before chemotherapy drugs
15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy; oral or intravenous bolus
15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy; oral or intravenous bolus
Cisplatin pre-hydration: furosemide 40mg oral or intravenous bolus, then 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride and 16mmol magnesium sulphate over 60 minutes

take_home_medicines.special_instructions: Starting on the evening of day 1 of treatment

dose_modifications.notes: 100% dose.
Delay chemotherapy until recovery then continue with 80% of the original dose.
100% dose.
Delay chemotherapy until recovery then continue with 80% of the original dose.
No dose adjustment needed (N/A for bilirubin/AST/ALT criteria).

dose_modifications.condition: Fluorouracil: bilirubin < 85 micromol/L and AST/ALT < 180 units
dose_modifications.notes: 100% fluorouracil dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Fluorouracil: bilirubin >= 85 micromol/L or AST/ALT >= 180 units
dose_modifications.notes: Fluorouracil is contra-indicated.
Consider dose modification.
100% cisplatin dose.
Consider alternative to cisplatin.
No dose adjustment needed (N/A).
100% streptozocin dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Streptozocin: < 60 ml/min or significant reduction from baseline
dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose reduction or omission of streptozocin.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance prior to each cycle. Consider blood transfusion if patient symptomatic of anaemia or has haemoglobin less than 8g/dL.

monitoring_requirements: FBC prior to each cycle
LFTs (including albumin) and U&Es prior to each cycle
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance prior to each cycle
Regular monitoring of blood glucose levels
Regular urinalysis for signs of proteinuria
All patients should be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation (cycle 1) to minimise the risk of severe and fatal toxicity with fluorouracil
special_warnings: Patients with complete or partial DPD deficiency are at increased risk of severe and fatal toxicity during treatment with fluorouracil. All patients should be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation.
Streptozocin is a vesicant.
Cisplatin is an exfoliant.
Fluorouracil is an inflammantant.

Cisplatin requires pre and post hydration: Pre - furosemide 40mg oral or IV bolus then 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol KCl and 16mmol magnesium sulphate over 60 minutes; Post - 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol KCl and 16mmol magnesium sulphate over 60 minutes.
Patients should be advised to drink at least 3 litres of fluid in the 24 hours after administration of cisplatin.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.

For all other non-haematological NCI-CTC grade 3 and above toxicities delay treatment until adverse effect resolves to grade 1 or below. Dose of causative agent should then be reduced to 80% of original dose or discontinued.

required_patient_fields.creatinine_clearance: Required for renal dose modification - EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance prior to each cycle; cisplatin contra-indicated if CrCl < 60ml/min
required_patient_fields.bilirubin: Required for hepatic dose modification; fluorouracil contra-indicated if bilirubin >= 85 micromol/L

drugs.special_instructions: Minimum 120 minutes. Maximum rate 1mg cisplatin/minute. Exfoliant. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (1mg/ml). Requires pre and post hydration.
drugs.notes: once | Minimum 120 minutes. Maximum rate 1mg cisplatin/minute. Exfoliant. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (1mg/ml). Requires pre and post hydration.

drugs.special_instructions: Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Inflammantant. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (25mg/ml PM). Patients should be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation.
drugs.notes: once | Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Inflammantant. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (25mg/ml PM). Patients should be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation.

drugs.special_instructions: Intravenous infusion in 250ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes.
drugs.notes: once | Intravenous infusion in 250ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes.

drugs.special_instructions: Vesicant. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (100mg/ml).
drugs.notes: once | Vesicant. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (100mg/ml).

indication: The 1st, 2nd or 3rd line treatment of pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinomas that are biopsy proven and well differentiated. No previous treatment with a VEGF inhibitor. WHO Performance status 0, 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Available as 12.5mg, 25mg, 37.5mg and 50mg capsules. Dose modifications should occur in 12.5mg steps. Daily dose should not be decreased below 25mg. In pNET dose should not exceed 50mg once a day.

dose_modifications.notes: 100% dose. Prior to cycle 1 neutrophils must be >= 1x10^9/L.
Delay until recovery to 1x10^9/L or greater. If recovery occurs within 7 days then continue with the last dose.

dose_modifications.condition: < 1.0 x10^9/L, recovery takes longer than 7 days
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose by 12.5mg (from 37.5mg to 25mg).
100% dose. Prior to cycle 1 platelets must be >= 75x10^9/L.
Delay until recovery to 75x10^9/L or greater. If recovery occurs within 7 days continue with full dose.

dose_modifications.condition: < 75 x10^9/L, recovery takes longer than 7 days
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose by 12.5mg.
37.5mg daily (pNET starting dose).
37.5mg daily (pNET). No information on dosing if AST or ALT > 2.5xULN (or > 5xULN with liver metastases) as these patients were excluded from clinical trials.
No information available.
No dose modification required for renal impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Hypertension persistently > 140/90mmHg despite standard antihypertensive therapy
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce sunitinib dose in 12.5mg steps and continue to monitor. If hypertension persists discontinue sunitinib.
For NCI-CTC grade 3 adverse reaction reduce dose by 12.5mg.
Stop sunitinib until resolves to at least NCI-CTC grade 2. Treatment may be re-started with a 12.5mg dose reduction.
Delay treatment until resolves to at least NCI-CTC grade 1. Sunitinib may be re-started with a 12.5mg dose reduction.
Delay until resolves to NCI-CTC grade 1. Sunitinib can be re-started with a 12.5mg dose reduction.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Prior to cycle 1: neutrophils >= 1x10^9/L and platelets >= 75x10^9/L must be met. 28 day cycle until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity occurs.

monitoring_requirements: FBCs, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle for the first three cycles, may reduce to every other cycle if stable
Blood pressure weekly for the first 4 weeks then every 6-12 weeks
Thyroid function tests at baseline then every 3 months

Ensure adequate cardiac function before starting therapy. Baseline LVEF should be measured in patients with history of cardiac problems or in the elderly. Repeat every 3-6 months as clinically indicated
special_warnings: Sunitinib should only be prescribed under the supervision of an oncologist.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed.
Daily dose should not be decreased below 25mg.
In pNET the dose of sunitinib should not exceed 50mg once a day.
Dose modifications should occur in 12.5mg steps.
Hypothyroidism can occur and should be managed according to standard medical practice. No need to discontinue or dose reduce sunitinib.
Hypertension should be treated initially as per NICE guidelines. For persistently high BP > 140/90mmHg despite standard therapy, reduce dose in 12.5mg steps. If hypertension persists discontinue.
Mouthcare for prophylaxis or treatment of mucositis in accordance with CSCCN guidelines.
Loperamide 4mg after first loose stool then 2-4mg four times a day when required for relief of diarrhoea (maximum 16mg/24 hours).
Consider blood transfusion if patient symptomatic of anaemia or has haemoglobin less than 8g/dL.

required_patient_fields.bilirubin: Required for hepatic dose modification (Child Pugh classification)

drugs.special_instructions: Available as 12.5mg, 25mg, 37.5mg and 50mg capsules. Dose modifications should occur in 12.5mg steps. Daily dose should not be decreased below 25mg. In pNET dose should not exceed 50mg once a day.
drugs.notes: once daily | Available as 12.5mg, 25mg, 37.5mg and 50mg capsules. Dose modifications should occur in 12.5mg steps. Daily dose should not be decreased below 25mg. In pNET dose should not exceed 50mg once a day.

indication: Unresectable, locally advanced or metastatic thyroid cancer where the disease is progressive and symptomatic and there has been no previous treatment with a tyrosine kinase inhibitor or intolerant to vandetanib (i.e. patients developing serious drug toxicity within 3 months of starting vandetanib which could not be managed with a dose delay or reduction and who have also NOT progressed on vandetanib)

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Cometriq (cabozantinib) brand licensed for thyroid cancer. Capsules should be swallowed whole and not opened. Patients should not eat for at least 2 hours before through 1 hour after taking capsules. Available as 20mg and 80mg capsules. If dose missed and less than 12 hours before next dose, do not take missed dose. Dose modifications in multiples of 20mg and 80mg capsule strengths.

take_home_medicines.special_instructions: Oral chemotherapy. Cometriq brand. Capsules swallowed whole, not opened. No food for at least 2 hours before through 1 hour after. Available as 80mg and 20mg capsules - ensure dose modifications occur in multiples of these strengths.

dose_modifications.notes: Neutrophils should generally be greater than 1.5x10^9/L and platelets greater than 100x10^9/L prior to each cycle.
Platelets should generally be greater than 100x10^9/L prior to each cycle.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 2 elevated ALT, AST or bilirubin for longer than 1 week
dose_modifications.notes: Dose reductions should be considered when grade 2 elevated ALT, AST or bilirubin for longer than 1 week occurs. First dose reduction: 100mg.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 and above elevated ALT, AST or bilirubin
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold treatment until resolved to baseline levels, then restart at lower dose.
No information available for renal impairment dose modification.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or greater toxicity, or intolerable grade 2 toxicity (first dose reduction)
dose_modifications.notes: Dose interruption required. Treatment may be resumed with first dose reduction: 100mg once daily, after toxicity improved to NCI-CTC grade 1.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or greater toxicity, or intolerable grade 2 toxicity (second dose reduction)
dose_modifications.notes: Second dose reduction: 60mg once daily, after toxicity improved to NCI-CTC grade 1.

Instigate antihypertensive treatment - increase dose of existing agents, add agents or commence therapy. For persistent hypertension > 150/100mmHg despite anti-hypertensives, consider cabozantinib dose reduction. If severe hypertension despite dose reduction and antihypertensives, discontinue.
Stop treatment for NCI-CTC grade 3 diarrhoea. Re-start with reduced dose once diarrhoea has subsided.
Discontinue cabozantinib in patients who develop nephrotic syndrome.
ECG QTc interval should not be greater than 480ms. Use with caution in patients with history of QT prolongation, anti-arrhythmics, bradycardia, or electrolyte disturbances.
Discontinue cabozantinib if PRES is diagnosed.
Discontinue treatment in patients who experience a GI perforation or a GI or non-GI fistula.

Discontinue cabozantinib in patients who experience ONJ. Oral examination should be performed prior to initiation and periodically during therapy. Hold at least 28 days prior to invasive dental procedures.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: 28 day cycle until disease progression or intolerance. Blood pressure should be well controlled prior to initiating cabozantinib. Serum calcitonin level should be greater than or equal to 500pg/ml.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of treatment (including magnesium, potassium, calcium and phosphate - abnormal levels should be corrected)
Thyroid function tests at baseline and 2, 4, 8 and 12 weeks and then every 12 weeks thereafter
ECG at baseline and 2, 4, 8 and 12 weeks and then every 12 weeks thereafter
Blood pressure prior to each cycle - existing hypertension should be well controlled prior to starting treatment
Urine dipstick for protein prior to every cycle
special_warnings: Cometriq (cabozantinib) brand is licensed for thyroid cancer. Cabometyx (cabozantinib) tablets and Cometriq capsules are NOT bioequivalent and should not be used interchangeably.
If patient must switch from capsules to tablets, continue at a Cabometyx dose not to exceed 60mg or the current Cometriq dose (whichever is lower).
Cabozantinib is associated with many drug interactions. Always check before prescribing.

QT prolongation: ECG QTc should not exceed 480ms. Use with caution in patients with congenital QT prolongation, anti-arrhythmics, bradycardia or electrolyte disturbances. Correct electrolyte abnormalities prior to starting.
PRES has been observed with cabozantinib - discontinue if PRES diagnosed.
Serious and sometimes fatal GI perforations, fistulas and intra-abdominal abscesses have been reported - discontinue if GI perforation or fistula occurs.
Osteonecrosis of the jaw - oral examination should be performed prior to initiation and periodically during therapy. Hold at least 28 days prior to invasive dental procedures.
Proteinuria - monitor urinary protein prior to each cycle. Discontinue if nephrotic syndrome develops.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed.
Patients should be assessed for suitability for oral chemotherapy prior to starting treatment.
Toxicity more commonly observed in patients aged 75 years or older.

required_patient_fields.creatinine_clearance: No formal renal dose modification available but monitor renal function

drugs.special_instructions: Cometriq (cabozantinib) brand licensed for thyroid cancer. Capsules should be swallowed whole and not opened. Patients should not eat for at least 2 hours before through 1 hour after taking capsules. Available as 20mg and 80mg capsules. If dose missed and less than 12 hours before next dose, do not take missed dose. Dose modifications in multiples of 20mg and 80mg capsule strengths.
drugs.notes: once daily | Cometriq (cabozantinib) brand licensed for thyroid cancer. Capsules should be swallowed whole and not opened. Patients should not eat for at least 2 hours before through 1 hour after taking capsules. Available as 20mg and 80mg capsules. If dose missed and less than 12 hours before next dose, do not take missed dose. Dose modifications in multiples of 20mg and 80mg capsule strengths.
